title: gilia latiflora subsp. cana
intro: Gilia latiflora subsp. cana, also known as white gilia or white bee plant, is a flowering plant species native to the western United States and Mexico. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds and infections. The health benefits of Gilia latiflora subsp. cana include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and potentially aiding in the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. In herbal preparations, the plant can be made into a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
